content.name: AIAP Creator v2.34.0 — Generator

output_mode.L0: Output JSON in ENGLISH: {intent, confidence, route, state, op}
output_mode.L1: Output helpfully in User's exact language and script variant

content.summary: Generates AISOP files with sub_mermaid architecture (scaffold/content/tooling). ThreeDimTest (C1-C7, I1-I13, D1-D10) with MF1-MF38 multi-file audit, FORMAT_DETECT for parsing, traffic light classification, I-SELF-REFERENTIAL scoring, TOOL-COMPUTED deterministic checks, semantic regression detection, fractal exempt drift verification, ASSERT_REQUIRED_ASSIGNMENT auto-generation for target programs. ~25K tokens/invocation.
content.description: Generator module with sub_mermaid architecture: generate_scaffold (AIAP.md, agent_card, safety_card), generate_content (AISOP output with per-function step generation), generate_tooling (tool directory generation). Supports Pattern A-G including multi-file generation. 30 nodes (26 functional). LoadChecklist ensures quality_checklist is loaded on Modified File path before ThreeDimTest. Version sync, governance hash, MF compliance, protocol config generation.

params.plan: object|null, default: null, Accept from Research Propose. Null if modifying existing. [agentic_prompt: Structured creation/evolution plan containing domain analysis, architecture decisions, and capability specifications — drives what the Generator builds.]
params.modified_file_path: string|null, default: null, Accept from ModifyFlow for re-test. Single file: file path string. Multi-file (Pattern B+): directory path — Generate will scan for all .aisop.json files within. [agentic_prompt: File or directory path to re-test after ModifyStep quality fixes — triggers LoadChecklist→ThreeDimTest path instead of full generation.]
params.workspace_dir: string, REQUIRED: [AIAP Store Directory] absolute path injected in system instruction. User override takes precedence. [agentic_prompt: Root directory for all AIAP program files — all file operations are scoped within this boundary for security.]
params.quality_threshold: string, default: standard, strict=A>=3.6, standard=B>=2.7, relaxed=C>=2.0 (weighted). [agentic_prompt: Controls ThreeDimTest pass/fail threshold — determines minimum weighted score required for QualityGate passage.]
params.evolution_plan: object|null, default: null, Accept from EvolveFlow. Contains confirmed LEVEL_A/B/C changes. Null if not evolution. [agentic_prompt: User-confirmed evolution changes from EvolveStep — Generator applies these as code modifications to the target AIAP program.]
params.evolution_context: object|null, default: null, Accept from Pipeline. Contains old_version_context for evolution verification. Null if Create mode or Modify-only. Schema per STANDARD.data_contracts.evolution_context. [agentic_prompt: Baseline state of the target program before evolution — used for diff verification and regression detection.]
params.module_hashes: object|null, default: null, Accept from Pipeline PipelineStart. Content hashes of target sub-module files for incremental generation. Keys are module_ids, values are SHA-256 hashes. Null if not Pattern B+ or not Evolve mode. [agentic_prompt: SHA-256 content hashes enabling incremental generation — unchanged modules are skipped to reduce token usage.]
params.wave_context: object|null, default: null, Provided by main.aisop.json Generate1 when wave-based generation is active. Contains { wave_index: number, wave_files: [string], total_waves: number, completed_waves: [{ wave_index, files, quality_score }] }. If null, single-pass generation (backward compatible). [agentic_prompt: Wave-based generation control for large programs (>6 files) — splits generation into sequential waves with inter-wave quality checks.]

content.system_prompt:
AISOP code generator and quality tester. Produce structurally valid output. Enforce Mermaid-function synchronization rigorously. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align Axiom 0: Human Sovereignty and Wellbeing.
content.fractal_exempt: 26 functional nodes distributed across 4 sub_mermaid sub-graphs (main 14 + generate_scaffold 3 + generate_content 6 + generate_tooling 3). Sub-graphs execute sequentially: scaffold → content → tooling. Maximum single-path execution 26 nodes (new plan path through all 3 sub-graphs).

threat_mapping_refs.ASI02: prompt injection guard - I2 input sanitization
threat_mapping_refs.ASI03: training data integrity - quality_baseline regression detection
threat_mapping_refs.ASI04: model supply chain - tool dependency pinning

aisop.main:
graph TD
    Start[Plan or Modified File] --> RouteGate{Route?}
    RouteGate -- New Plan --> ReadTemplate[Read AISOP Template]
    RouteGate -- Modified File --> LoadChecklist[Load Quality Checklist]
    LoadChecklist --> ThreeDimTest[Three-Dimensional Test]
    ReadTemplate --> GenerateScaffold[aisop.generate_scaffold]
    GenerateScaffold --> GenerateContent[aisop.generate_content]
    GenerateContent --> GenerateTooling[aisop.generate_tooling]
    GenerateTooling --> ThreeDimTest
    ThreeDimTest --> TrafficClassify[Traffic Light Classification]
    TrafficClassify --> PostGenCheck{Unresolved Red?}
    PostGenCheck -- Yes --> PauseHuman[Pause for Human]
    PostGenCheck -- No --> ApplyFixes[Apply Green+Yellow Fixes]
    PauseHuman -- Approved --> ApplyFixes
    PauseHuman -- Rejected --> SaveDraft[Save Draft with Warnings]
    ApplyFixes --> SaveDraft
    SaveDraft --> ReflectionCheck[Reflection Check]
    ReflectionCheck --> endNode((End))
aisop.generate_scaffold:
graph TD
    ScaffoldStart[Scaffold Input] --> PrepareAIAPMd[Prepare AIAP.md + License + Security]
    PrepareAIAPMd --> GenerateAgentCard[Generate Agent Card + Safety Card]
    GenerateAgentCard --> endNode((End))
aisop.generate_content:
graph TD
    ContentStart[Content Input] --> GenerateAISOP[Generate AISOP Files]
    GenerateAISOP --> EvolutionVerify[Evolution Verification]
    EvolutionVerify --> MermaidSync[Mermaid Sync + Impact Scan]
    MermaidSync --> MFAudit[Multi-File Audit]
    MFAudit --> FractalCheck[Fractal + Pattern Check]
    FractalCheck --> endNode((End))
aisop.generate_tooling:
graph TD
    ToolingStart[Tooling Input] --> GenerateUI[UI Generation]
    GenerateUI --> GenerateToolDirs[Tool Directory Generation]
    GenerateToolDirs --> endNode((End))

Start.step1: [ASSERT] Valid input received. Required: plan, modified_file_path.  | Receive plan or modified_file_path. Pass to RouteGate.
Start.constraints: (A) Type guard — string/object only. (B) Size guard — plan >500KB -> reject. (C) Injection guard — sanitize paths. (D) Path guard — reject any path not resolving within workspace_dir (including absolute paths outside workspace and '..' traversal); validate canonical path via realpath/resolve before acceptance. (E) Encoding guard — NFC normalize. (F) BOUNDARY_CONSISTENCY: All params with type annotations must have values conforming to declared type at entry - type mismatch -> reject with TYPE_BOUNDARY_VIOLATION. (G) NULL_SAFETY: Required params null -> reject immediately; optional params null -> use default. (H) SYMLINK_GUARD: reject symbolic links pointing outside workspace_dir boundary.
Start.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(RouteGate) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback. validation_failure(type_boundary_violation)->no retry, reject immediately.

RouteGate.step1: [ASSERT] Start executed. Required: plan, modified_file_path.  | Route: plan non-null -> ReadTemplate. modified_file_path non-null -> LoadChecklist.
RouteGate.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(ReadTemplate) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

LoadChecklist.step1: [ASSERT] RouteGate executed. Required: modified_file_path, quality_checklist_path.  | QUALITY CHECKLIST LOAD (Modified File path only): Load AIAP_Standard.core.aisop.json from pipeline_context.creator_directory (fallback: directory of this generate.aisop.json). Extract quality_checklist + extension_registry. This ensures ThreeDimTest has criteria definitions before scoring.
LoadChecklist.step2: EXTENSION LOADING: (1) ALWAYS load security extension, (2) ecosystem if Pattern>=B, (3) performance if pipeline or strict mode. Merge extension checklists into quality_checklist context for ThreeDimTest consumption.
LoadChecklist.constraints: MANDATORY before ThreeDimTest on Modified File path. quality_checklist MUST be loaded and available in context before any scoring occurs. [PROHIBIT]: No scoring without reading criteria definition first.
LoadChecklist.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(ThreeDimTest) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

ReadTemplate.step1: [ASSERT] RouteGate executed. Required: plan, quality_checklist_path.  | TEMPLATE DISCOVERY: Load AIAP_Standard.core.aisop.json from pipeline_context.creator_directory (fallback: directory of this generate.aisop.json). Extract quality_checklist + extension_registry. EXTENSION LOADING: (1) ALWAYS load security extension, (2) ecosystem if Pattern>=B, (3) performance if pipeline or strict mode. Merge extension checklists. Verify template_version match.
ReadTemplate.step2: Read AIAP_Protocol.md from same directory. Apply Pattern selection: 1 function -> A, 2+ -> B, 2+ with shared logic -> C, sub-modules -> D. For Pattern G programs with aiap_tools/: load I13 sub-checks (g)(h)(i)(j) and MF16 sub-checks (e)(f)(g)(h) from STANDARD.core and STANDARD.ecosystem for aiap_tools validation.
ReadTemplate.step3: Extract complexity + archetype from plan. Finalize generation strategy.
ReadTemplate.step4: PROMPT TEMPLATE MANAGEMENT: read prompt_templates.json from aisop_creator directory (same location as AIAP_Standard.core.aisop.json). If found, load domain-appropriate templates: system_prompt templates, Error field format templates, boundary rule templates, constraints templates. Apply loaded templates during generation for consistency. If prompt_templates.json not found, use built-in defaults — this is expected for most installations. Template format: { templates: { system_prompt: string, error_field: string, boundary_rule: string, constraints: string }, domain_overrides: { [domain]: { ...partial template overrides } } }.
ReadTemplate.constraints: Template not found -> use AI knowledge as fallback. Discovery priority: (1) same directory as generate.aisop.json, (2) scan workspace_dir subdirectories, (3) AI knowledge. (D) Path guard — validate discovered paths within workspace_dir, reject '..'.
ReadTemplate.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(GenerateScaffold) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

GenerateScaffold.step1: [ASSERT] ReadTemplate executed. Required: plan, template_data, extension_registry, evolution_plan.  | DELEGATE to aisop.generate_scaffold. Pass plan, template data, and evolution_plan to scaffold sub-graph for metadata and structural file generation. DELEGATION_PARAMS_VERIFIED: all required target module params explicitly passed per I6 contract.
GenerateScaffold.constraints: Sequential dependency: scaffold output (AIAP.md, agent_card.json) is consumed by generate_content.
GenerateScaffold.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(GenerateContent) -> inform(User). IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_write_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

GenerateContent.step1: [ASSERT] GenerateScaffold executed. Required: scaffold_output, plan, template, evolution_plan, wave_context.  | DELEGATE to aisop.generate_content. Pass scaffold output (AIAP.md metadata, agent_card data) plus plan, template, evolution_plan, module_hashes, wave_context to content sub-graph for core AISOP file generation. DELEGATION_PARAMS_VERIFIED: all required target module params explicitly passed per I6 contract.
GenerateContent.constraints: Sequential dependency: content output (.aisop.json files) is consumed by generate_tooling.
GenerateContent.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(GenerateTooling) -> inform(User). IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_write_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

GenerateTooling.step1: [ASSERT] GenerateContent executed. Required: content_output, aiap_md.  | DELEGATE to aisop.generate_tooling. Pass content output (generated .aisop.json files, AIAP.md) to tooling sub-graph for UI and tool directory generation. DELEGATION_PARAMS_VERIFIED: all required target module params explicitly passed per I6 contract.
GenerateTooling.constraints: Final generation stage. Output feeds into ThreeDimTest.
GenerateTooling.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(ThreeDimTest) -> inform(User). IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_write_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

ScaffoldStart.step1: [ASSERT] Valid input received. Required: plan, template_data, extension_registry, prompt_templates, evolution_plan.  | Receive plan, template data (quality_checklist, extension_registry, prompt_templates), and evolution_plan. Prepare generation context for scaffold files.
ScaffoldStart.constraints: (A) Type guard — validate plan/template structure. (B) Evolution mode: if evolution_plan provided, load existing AIAP.md for incremental update.
ScaffoldStart.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(PrepareAIAPMd) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

PrepareAIAPMd.step1: [ASSERT] ScaffoldStart executed. Required: scaffold_context.  | AIAP.md GENERATION: 14 required frontmatter fields (6 governance + 8 project), governance statement, feature overview, usage instructions, AIAP closing seal. Status='draft' for new. FLOW_FORMAT: always 'mermaid'.
PrepareAIAPMd.step2: LICENSE & AUTHORSHIP: (A) LICENSE — SPDX identifier, default 'proprietary'. If proprietary, require terms_url or contact. Evolve: preserve unless user changes. (B) AUTHOR — prompt, empty string if skipped (don't omit field). (C) COPYRIGHT — same pattern as author.
PrepareAIAPMd.step3: FRONTMATTER EXTENSION: include skills[], capabilities, authentication from STANDARD extended_frontmatter_fields. Add missing fields with defaults.
PrepareAIAPMd.step4: SECURITY SCHEMES: T1→none, T2→bearer, T3→oauth2, T4→oauth2+mutualTls. GOVERNANCE_HASH: Create mode → placeholder 'pending — computed in ReviewFinalize'. FLOW_FORMAT: always 'mermaid' in frontmatter. SEED: always 'aisop.dev'.
PrepareAIAPMd.constraints: AIAP.md PREPARED but not written — passed to ReviewFinalize for single-ownership write. License defaults to 'proprietary'.
PrepareAIAPMd.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(GenerateAgentCard) -> inform(User). IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_write_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

GenerateAgentCard.step1: [ASSERT] PrepareAIAPMd executed. Required: aiap_md_metadata.  | AGENT CARD (UNCONDITIONAL): generate agent_card.json per STANDARD — name, description (first 200 chars of summary), version, provider, pattern, tags, skills[] from NLU intents, capabilities (default all-false), trust_level, securitySchemes (mapped by trust level), tools, modules, status. Add _generated metadata. Write to {workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/agent_card.json. Atomic write. Evolve: regenerate from updated AIAP.md.
GenerateAgentCard.step2: SAFETY CARD (UNCONDITIONAL): Within agent_card.json — risk_level (from trust_level, elevate for crisis/medical/financial), data_handling (from memory module or defaults), limitations (from description), compliance_markers (from Research3 if available).
GenerateAgentCard.constraints: All programs get agent_card.json + safety_card. Atomic write.
GenerateAgentCard.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(ContentStart) -> inform(User). IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_write_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

ContentStart.step1: [ASSERT] Valid input received. Required: scaffold_output, plan, template, evolution_plan, module_hashes, wave_context.  | Receive scaffold output (AIAP.md metadata, agent_card data) plus plan, template, evolution_plan, module_hashes, wave_context. Prepare generation context for AISOP file generation.
ContentStart.constraints: (A) Verify scaffold output contains required AIAP.md metadata. (B) If scaffold was skipped (fallback), derive metadata from plan directly.
ContentStart.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(GenerateAISOP) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

GenerateAISOP.step1: [ASSERT] ContentStart executed. Required: content_context.  | NODE BUDGET ADVISORY (Create only): Pattern A=1 module 6-12 nodes, Pattern B=2-3 modules 6-14 each, Pattern D/F=3-8 modules 6-14 each. Per-module >14 → YELLOW, >16 → RED. TOOL ANNOTATIONS: generate read_only/destructive/idempotent/open_world per tool. JSON SCHEMA: auto-generate output schema per module from params/outputs/contracts. Generate AISOP: system.content + Mermaid + functions. Apply fractal pattern. NULL HANDLING: ensure null-safe fallback for optional inputs.
GenerateAISOP.step2: MULTI-FILE: affected sub-modules bottom-up, main last. Cross-file data contracts consistent. INCREMENTAL: if module_hashes provided, skip unchanged modules (all 4 conditions: hash unchanged, template unchanged, quality_threshold unchanged, no dependency changes). Pattern E: memory module change forces regeneration of all memory-reading modules. Cache safety: strict mode disables incremental skip. Track decisions in pipeline_metadata.incremental_decisions.
GenerateAISOP.step3: METADATA-ONLY MODE: when evolution_plan.metadata_only_mode=true (all changes target AIAP.md only), update only name/version/description in .aisop.json files — preserve all functional content. Auto-detect if evolution_plan changes target only AIAP_MD modules.
GenerateAISOP.step4: WAVE-BASED GENERATION: if wave_context provided, generate only wave_context.wave_files. Use completed_waves for cross-file stubs. Inter-wave quality: C+D dimensions only (skip I). Retain actual tier. If wave_context null → single-pass.
GenerateAISOP.step5: OPERATION VOCABULARY: prefer standard verbs (RUN, DELEGATE, ROUTE, GATE, COLLECT, EMIT, CLASSIFY) in generated steps. Recommended not required. MULTIMODAL INFERENCE: set inputModes/outputModes in skills[] based on tools (image_generation→image/png, tts→audio/wav, etc).
GenerateAISOP.step6: A2A BRIDGE CONFIG: Pattern F/G + trust_level>=T3 → generate bridge_config (enabled, interfaces, auth, timeout). Delegate to A2AAdvisor. Skip for Pattern A-E without opt-in or T<3. DELEGATION_PARAMS_VERIFIED: all required target module params explicitly passed per I6 contract.
GenerateAISOP.step7: EXTENSION RECOMMENDATION: Pattern E → memory-tier, safety modules → safety-screening, 5+ intents → intent-vectors. Advisory only, user must declare in AIAP.md.
GenerateAISOP.step8: LOADING_MODE INJECTION: For every generated .aisop.json file, inject 'loading_mode': 'normal' into system.content. This is a REQUIRED protocol field per AIAP_Protocol §2.5. Default 'normal' (all functions loaded at once). Alternative 'node' (on-demand per-node loading) or 'lite' (direct execution without engine) is only set when user explicitly requests it. Verify: every generated .aisop.json contains loading_mode field — missing → auto-inject with 'normal' and log 'LOADING_MODE: auto-injected normal for {file}'. Generated programs declare their loading_mode per the protocol; how a particular runtime implements each mode is out of scope for generation.
GenerateAISOP.step10: ASSERT_REQUIRED_ASSIGNMENT: For every generated .aisop.json file, auto-assign [ASSERT] Required clauses to node functions. Rules: (a) For each function with [ASSERT] {PredecessorNode} in any step text, analyze the predecessor node's function definition to identify its output fields and execution record structure. (b) Identify which data fields the current function's step text references from the predecessor — scan for field names, parameter names, and data flow patterns that trace back to predecessor output. (c) Inject 'Required: {field1, field2, ...}' clause into the [ASSERT] statement, listing the specific data dependencies identified. Format: '[ASSERT] {PredecessorNode} executed. Required: {field1, field2, ...}.' (d) If function references no predecessor data beyond execution status, use minimal Required clause (e.g., 'Required: pipeline_context'). (e) Verify: every [ASSERT] has a Required clause — missing Required → auto-inject with analyzed fields and log 'ASSERT_REQUIRED: auto-assigned Required for {function} based on data flow analysis from {predecessor}'. (f) Cross-validate: each Required field should exist in the predecessor's output schema, known execution record fields, or documented data contracts. Flag unresolvable fields as YELLOW with 'ASSERT_REQUIRED: unresolvable field {field} in {function} Required clause — predecessor {predecessor} output schema does not contain this field'.
GenerateAISOP.step11: MERMAID FLOW GENERATION: Generate Mermaid flowchart for §4 topology. Flow graph uses 'graph TD' format with node shapes and edge types per Mermaid flowchart subset. Instruction: 'RUN aisop.main'. All programs use Mermaid flow format (.aisop.json). SYS_CALL_ASSIGNMENT: After generating step text for each function, scan all step values for sys.* prefixed calls. For each detected sys.* call: (a) classify blocking behavior per AIAP_Standard.runtime_extensions.sys_call_spec.blocking_classification — forced_blocking (sys.io.confirm, sys.io.input, sys.io.select), blocking (sys.run, sys.io.read, sys.io.write, sys.code.exec, sys.llm, etc.), non_blocking (sys.io.notify, sys.run.bg, sys.event.emit, etc.). (b) Verify the sys.* namespace is valid (one of the 8 defined namespaces with 24 total calls per sys_call_spec). Invalid namespace → YELLOW with suggestion. (c) Verify step text contains exactly one classification mode per RE5 (sys.* call OR RUN aisop.* OR natural language — no mixing). Mixed-mode step → FAIL. (d) Record sys_call_metadata per generated file: { total_sys_calls, by_namespace: { sys.io: N, sys.run: N, ... }, by_blocking: { forced_blocking: N, blocking: N, non_blocking: N }, calls: [{ step, call, namespace, blocking_class }] }. Pass sys_call_metadata downstream for ValidateStep (B2) and ReviewPresent (B3) consumption.
GenerateAISOP.step13: GENERATION_HYGIENE (pre-write scan): Before finalizing generated output, perform inline nihil density pre-check. Scan generated step text and constraint text for FILLER patterns (generic phrases per STANDARD.generation_hygiene.nihil_density.categories.FILLER), PLACEHOLDER patterns (TODO/TBD/FIXME), and VACUOUS_CONSTRAINT patterns (constraints imposing no verifiable restriction). Remove detected filler in-place before writing. Log removed items: { removed_count, removed_chars, categories }. This is a lightweight pre-generation hygiene pass; full nihil density analysis occurs in NihilDensityStep post-generation.
GenerateAISOP.step14: SCHEMA_PRECHECK: For each generated *.aisop.json file, before writeCache, perform strict schema validation against AIAP_Standard.core.aisop.json quality_baseline schema + protocol REQUIRED fields. (a) REQUIRED FIELDS CHECK: load STANDARD.quality_baseline.required + protocol-level required top-level fields (protocol, id, version, trust_level, loading_mode, tools, system_prompt, fractal_exempt, output_mode, json_schema). Assert each exists in the generated content; missing → FAIL with specific field name, do NOT writeCache — loop back to step1 to inject. (b) TYPE MASK CHECK: validate field types per AIAP_Standard: trust_level=number, version=string matching ^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$, token_estimate=string, loading_mode=enum[normal,node,lite]. Any type mismatch → FAIL with field+expected+actual, coerce+retry once (int→string for token_estimate is safe), else halt. (c) MULTI-FILE CONSISTENCY (Pattern B+): assert trust_level matches across all generated module files. (d) LOG: schema_precheck_result = { files_checked, passed, failed:[{file, field, reason}], auto_fixed:[{file, field, from, to}] }. [PROHIBIT]: writeCache if any REQUIRED field missing AND auto-injection failed.
GenerateAISOP.constraints: Apply quality_checklist from STANDARD. Evolution items dropped → flag warning. MF4 contracts for new cross-module refs. Complexity thresholds: DECOMPOSITION PLAN if exceeded. CREATE MODE: auto-generate data contracts for each communicating module pair. GENERATION_HYGIENE: pre-write filler removal is best-effort, non-blocking. SCHEMA_PRECHECK (step14): BLOCKING — any REQUIRED field missing after auto-injection attempt halts Generate1 and returns to ModifyStep for explicit fix. Type coercion (int→string for token_estimate) is allowed; semantic fixes (missing trust_level) require value decision from evolution_plan or parent module. SUMMARY_GENERATION_RULE (P28, B3 v2.9.0 hardened): system.content.summary MUST be <=500 chars AND describe current capabilities/features only. It MUST NOT include evolution changelog history (no 'v1.X.Y->v1.X.Y+1: ...' segments, no 'B1/B2/...' per-cycle change lists). Changelog belongs in AIAP.md and CHANGELOG.md, not summary. Evolve mode: if the old summary contains inherited changelog segments, strip them before applying current-cycle edits; do not accumulate. SUMMARY AUTO-TRUNCATION ENFORCEMENT: after generating or editing summary, measure len(summary). If len > 500: (1) strip changelog patterns via regex /v\d+\.\d+\.\d+\s*->\s*v\d+\.\d+\.\d+[^.]*\./g, (2) if still > 500, drop trailing capability references sentence-by-sentence (never mid-sentence) until len <= 500, (3) if still > 500 after sentence removal, compress adjectives and parenthetical details, (4) BLOCKING — do NOT proceed to writeCache if summary > 500 chars after 3 compression attempts; return to step1 for manual rewrite. Log: { original_length, compressed_length, method_used, sentences_dropped }. | VERIFIED_PRESENT_PROOF (Cycle 234 finding): If during evolution plan application you decide an A/B item is already satisfied by the current codebase (i.e. no modification needed, status 'verified_present'), you MUST provide EVIDENCE in the item's status record: (1) `grep_output`: the actual grep/Read output line(s) showing the feature/text that proves presence; (2) `diff_bytes: 0`: explicit confirmation no bytes changed; (3) `evidence_cite`: file:line_number pair(s) where the feature already exists. Prose claims like 'verified_present, already exists in v<N>' WITHOUT grep evidence are FABRICATION RISK — the LLM may be skipping the check to save tokens. ValidateStep should cross-check verified_present claims against grep_output content. If grep_output is empty or evidence_cite missing, treat the item as 'unverified_skip' and raise WARNING.
GenerateAISOP.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(EvolutionVerify) -> inform(User). IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_write_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

EvolutionVerify.step1: [ASSERT] GenerateAISOP executed. Required: generated_aisop, evolution_plan, old_version_context.  | If evolution_plan provided, implement LEVEL_A (structural), LEVEL_B (functional), LEVEL_C (incremental) changes. VERIFICATION: cross-check each change against evolution_context.old_version. Flag unverifiable items. ROUTING PARAM AUTO-CREATION: when LEVEL_A adds sub-module, copy routing parameter pattern from existing sub-modules. NLU CONFUSION MATRIX: new intent needs >=2 confusion pairs with existing intents. TIER-APPROPRIATE: Simple/Standard → add steps within functions; Professional → may add nodes/routes. FIX REGRESSION PROTECTION: when rewriting constraints/Error, diff old vs new — preserve more precise/qualified old wording if new text generalizes.
EvolutionVerify.step2: DEGRADED ITEM DETECTION (Evolve only): scan evolution_context.old_version for items removed or weakened. Categories: (A) REMOVED NODES — old Mermaid nodes missing in new. (B) WEAKENED CONSTRAINTS — old constraint text more specific than new. (C) DROPPED ERROR HANDLING — old Error more comprehensive. (D) PARAMETER REGRESSION — old params had tighter types/defaults. For each degraded item: { item, category, old_text_snippet, severity: INTENTIONAL|REGRESSION }. INTENTIONAL if evolution_plan explicitly removes it. REGRESSION if not in plan → YELLOW. Output degraded_items[] for TrafficClassify.
EvolutionVerify.constraints: All evolution_plan items must be verified. No silent drops.
EvolutionVerify.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(MermaidSync) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

MermaidSync.step1: [ASSERT] EvolutionVerify executed. Required: verified_aisop.  | MERMAID↔FUNCTIONS SYNC: verify every Mermaid node has a matching function and vice versa. Detect orphans (function without node) and phantoms (node without function). Auto-fix: add missing function stubs or remove orphan functions.
MermaidSync.step2: EVOLUTION IMPACT SCAN (Evolve only): compare old/new Mermaid graphs. Detect added/removed/renamed nodes. Verify all renamed nodes have updated function references. Flag any structural changes not in evolution_plan.
MermaidSync.step3: OUTPUT SCHEMA: MermaidSync output MUST conform to json_schema: { sync_result: { orphans: [{function_name: string, file: string}], phantoms: [{node_name: string, mermaid_graph: string}], auto_fixed: [{type: 'stub_added'|'orphan_removed', target: string}], sync_status: 'CLEAN'|'FIXED'|'RED' }, evolution_impact: { added_nodes: [string], removed_nodes: [string], renamed_nodes: [{old: string, new: string}], unplanned_changes: [string] } | null }. Downstream consumers (MFAudit, FractalCheck) type-check sync_result fields.
MermaidSync.constraints: Sync is mandatory — any Mermaid↔function mismatch is RED. Output MUST conform to step3 json_schema.
MermaidSync.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(MFAudit) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

MFAudit.step1: [ASSERT] MermaidSync executed. Required: synced_aisop.  | MULTI-FILE AUDIT (Pattern B+ only): Run MF1-MF27 + MF-INSIGHTS per ThreeDimTest.step6 definitions. Additional CREATE mode checks — POST-GENERATE MF PRECHECK: (1) MF20-EARLY: scan all module steps for cross-module call patterns, extract caller/target/params. (2) PARAMETER MATCH: verify all required target params are passed. Missing → RED. (3) PARAMETER NAME ALIGNMENT: edit distance check — <=2 YELLOW drift, no match RED. Auto-fix if distance <=4. (4) MF1-QUICK: verify all files have identical version. Apply to BOTH Create and Evolve modes. SKIP for Pattern A. Output: mf_audit = { mf1..mf27: PASS/FAIL, mf_insights: PASS/FAIL/N_A, precheck_fixes: [] }.
MFAudit.constraints: Any MF FAIL → log as generation_warning. Pattern B+ only, skip for Pattern A.
MFAudit.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(FractalCheck) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

FractalCheck.step1: [ASSERT] MFAudit executed. Required: audit_results.  | FRACTAL CHECK per STANDARD.C5: Count functional nodes per file (Mermaid - Start - endNode). PATTERN: 2+ independent functions → Pattern B. PROGRESSIVE: 13-15 → ADVISORY, 16+ → RECOMMENDED with split plan. Respect fractal_exempt. SHARED: 2+ modules reuse complex ops → Pattern C. Pattern E/F delegation to advisor. COMPOUND B+E: main=NLU router, sub-modules=functional, memory/=persistent. SUB_MERMAID CHECK: verify node counts per §7.1.4. FUNCTION BODY COMPLEXITY: 8+ steps → ADVISORY, 15+ directives → ADVISORY, 20+ → RECOMMENDED. Report pattern, node count, sub_mermaid status, body complexity.
FractalCheck.constraints: Progressive warnings only, never FAIL on node count alone. Respect fractal_exempt.
FractalCheck.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(ThreeDimTest) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

ToolingStart.step1: [ASSERT] Valid input received. Required: content_output, aiap_md_metadata.  | Receive content output (generated .aisop.json files, AIAP.md metadata). Prepare context for tooling generation.
ToolingStart.constraints: If no ui block and no tool_dirs in AIAP.md, both GenerateUI and GenerateToolDirs will skip (conditional execution).
ToolingStart.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(GenerateUI) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

GenerateUI.step1: [ASSERT] ToolingStart executed. Required: tooling_context.  | UI GENERATION (conditional): if target AIAP.md declares a 'ui' block, generate ui_output per STANDARD.data_contracts.ui_output contract. For each component in ui.components: (a) validate data_source references: (i) a known STANDARD data_contract name (e.g., pipeline_metadata, research_context, simulation_report, quality_baseline), OR (ii) a pipeline_metadata sub-field (e.g., pipeline_metadata.token_tracking), OR (iii) a program-internal data source — check target program's module outputs for matching field names or data flow paths (e.g., a module's function output that feeds the ui component via Mermaid data flow). For program-internal sources, verify the producing module exists in AIAP.md modules[] and the referenced data path is traceable through the program's function steps, (b) for type='form', validate fields[] have name+type+default, (b2) for type='form', verify each field name maps to a writable program data path (parameter with file_system persistence or profile field) rather than a runtime-only parameter; flag runtime-only fields as UI_DATA_MISMATCH warning, (c) set rendering_engine from ui.rendering (default 'mcp_apps_v1'), (d) set interactivity=true if any component type='form'. Skip entirely if no ui block (backward compatible). Output ui_output object validated against json_schema.
GenerateUI.constraints: Skip entirely if no ui block (backward compatible). Output ui_output object validated against json_schema.
GenerateUI.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(GenerateToolDirs) -> inform(User). IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_write_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

GenerateToolDirs.step1: [ASSERT] GenerateUI executed. Required: ui_output.  | TOOL DIRECTORY GENERATION (Pattern G only). If AIAP.md contains tool_dirs field: (a) ANALYZE program tools[] and capabilities to determine required tool implementations, (b) SELECT RUNTIME — recommend language per need: Python (data/AI/ML), TypeScript (Web API/type safety), Go (concurrency/CLI), Rust (performance/WASM), Shell (system scripts/lightweight), (c) GENERATE SKELETON per selected language: README.md (purpose, interface, security constraints), dependency file (requirements.txt / package.json / go.mod / Cargo.toml / none), tool implementation stubs (entry script + function stubs with docstrings), interface definition (CLI args or JSON stdin/stdout), (d) GENERATE MCP ADAPTER — create mcp_tools/mcp_adapter.{ext} (stdio JSON-RPC 2.0 bridge: read stdin line-by-line, parse JSON, route to tool function by params.name, return JSON response, handle errors with code -32601/-32603), (e) GENERATE mcp_server.json — { schema_version: 'mcp-1.0', server_id, transport: 'stdio', runtime, entry_point, exposed_tools: [from tools[]], file_hashes: {SHA-256 per file}, governance: { aiap_protocol, trust_level, governance_hash: '(computed by ReviewFinalize)' } }, (f) GENERATE agent_card.json (A2A v1.0 optional) — { name, description, capabilities, endpoint }, (g) I13 PRE-CHECK — validate dependency pinning (no ^ or ~ prefixes), network declarations, file scope within tool_dirs/. If non-Pattern-G program: skip with note 'non-Pattern-G, no tool_dirs generation needed.'
GenerateToolDirs.constraints: Pattern G only. If non-Pattern-G program: skip with note. I13 PRE-CHECK required.
GenerateToolDirs.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(ThreeDimTest) -> inform(User). IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_write_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

ThreeDimTest.step1: [ASSERT] quality_checklist loaded. Required: draft_path, quality_checklist.  If quality_checklist not in context → HALT and load from AIAP_Standard.core.aisop.json before proceeding. [PROHIBIT]: No scoring without reading criteria definition first. Each check item MUST have tool-verified evidence (file_system read, deterministic comparison), not cognitive-only scoring. On Modified File path, quality_checklist is loaded by LoadChecklist node. On New Plan path, quality_checklist is loaded by ReadTemplate node. [INDEPENDENT_AGENT] This node MUST be executed by an INDEPENDENT Agent that does NOT share context with the code-writing Agent. The scoring Agent receives ONLY: workspace_dir + AIAP_Standard.core.aisop.json path. It does NOT know modification history. Cognitive scores max 4.5/5.
ThreeDimTest.step2: [ASSERT] step1 executed (quality_checklist loaded). Required: draft_path, quality_checklist.  | TIER DETECTION: Simple (Pattern A, no file_system, <=6 nodes), Standard (Pattern A >6 nodes OR Pattern B OR file_system), Professional (Pattern C/D+, multi-file). AUXILIARY MODULE TEMPLATE: Pattern B+ sub_mermaid sub-graphs invoked only via 'run aisop.{key}' = auxiliary. Auxiliary scoring: C1-C4, I1-I5, D1-D5 only (rest N/A). Weight: C×0.30+I×0.40+D×0.30. Auxiliary weight=0.15 per module, cap 0.30 total; primary weight >= 0.70. CLASSIFICATION_REUSE: use pipeline_metadata.module_classification if available before re-scanning.
ThreeDimTest.step3: COGNITIVE C1-C7 (Professional weights C×0.35): C1 NAMING — identifiers self-describing, PascalCase nodes, snake_case params. C2 STRUCTURE — Mermaid→functions 1:1, no orphans/phantoms, M_SCORE coupling (threshold 3.0, >3.0→YELLOW). C3 FLOW — DAG validity, reachable endNode, no dead branches. C4 MODULARITY — SRP per node, cohesion. C5 FRACTAL — nodes per file <=16, sub_mermaid if needed, function body <=8 steps/<=15 directives advisory. C6 GLOBAL NAMING — cross-module consistency (Pattern B+). C7 L0 COMPLIANCE — output_mode.L0 present and valid JSON template. Score each 1-5.
ThreeDimTest.step4: INTRINSIC I1-I13 (Professional weights I×0.40): I1 INPUT VALIDATION — params type/default/guard. I2 SECURITY — injection/path traversal/encoding guards (5 items if file_system, 4 if not). I3 ERROR — retry→circuit-breaker→fallback→inform chain. I4 CONSTRAINTS — meaningful, non-empty, actionable per function. I5 ASSERT — 'ASSERT RUN aisop.main' present. I6 CROSS-MODULE — delegation params match target (Pattern B+). I7 PROTOCOL — AIAP compliance (§sections). I8 TOOL ANNOTATIONS — read_only/destructive/idempotent/open_world per tool. I9 DATA CONTRACTS — producer→consumer field match (Pattern B+). I10 json_schema — output schema present per module. I11 LOADING_MODE — loading_mode (valid: normal/node/lite) field present in system.content.content, values match program characteristics. I12 PROTOCOL CONFIG — domain protocol integration if applicable. I13 TOOL SECURITY — I13(a-f) standard, I13(g-j) Pattern G only: dependency pinning, network declarations, file scope, review triggers. I-SELF-REFERENTIAL: if testing Creator itself, apply self-referential scoring adjustment. Score each 1-5.
ThreeDimTest.step5: DETAIL D1-D10 (Professional weights D×0.25): D1 STEPS — each function has step1+, actionable language. D2 CONSTRAINT ALIGNMENT — constraints reference step behavior. D3 COVERAGE — Error fields cover failure modes. D4 SEMANTIC CONSISTENCY — step↔constraint no contradiction. D5 endNode — all paths reach endNode. D6 VERSION SYNC — version field matches AIAP.md, governance_hash present. D7 AGENT CARD — agent_card.json exists and matches AIAP.md. D8 SUMMARY — <=500 chars, accurate. D9 CROSS-MODULE (Pattern B+) — MF compliance. D10 DESCRIPTION — accurate capability description. TOOL-COMPUTED CHECKS: D6 governance_hash, D7 agent_card consistency, MF6 node count — use deterministic comparison not subjective judgment. Score each 1-5.
ThreeDimTest.step6: SCORING: Per-dimension average, then weighted: Professional C×0.35+I×0.40+D×0.25, Standard C×0.35+I×0.35+D×0.30, Simple C×0.40+I×0.30+D×0.30. Grade: S>=4.50, A>=3.50, B>=2.50, C>=1.50, D<1.50. Output: { dimension_scores: {C:{c1..c7}, I:{i1..i13}, D:{d1..d10}}, weighted_score, grade, tier }. SEMANTIC REGRESSION DETECTION (Evolve only): if evolution_context.old_version provided, compare old scores dimension-by-dimension. Any dimension drop >0.3 → YELLOW regression warning. FRACTAL EXEMPT DRIFT: if fractal_exempt declared, verify current node counts still match declared counts.
ThreeDimTest.step7: FRAMEWORK COMPLETENESS AUDIT: (1) TEMPLATE COVERAGE — verify quality_checklist items from AIAP_Standard all checked. (2) EXTENSION COVERAGE — verify loaded extensions applied. (3) AGENT CARD COMPLETENESS — verify agent_card.json fields populated. (4) SAFETY CARD — verify safety_card populated if trust_level>=T2. (5) PROTOCOL COMPLIANCE — verify protocol-specific requirements met. (6) DATA CONTRACT COMPLETENESS — verify all cross-module data flows have explicit contracts. Output framework_completeness: {checked, passed, gaps[]}.
ThreeDimTest.step8: MULTI-FILE AGGREGATE (Pattern B+ only): Run MF1-MF38 checks across all generated files. MF1 version consistency, MF2 delegation target match, MF3 shared param types, MF4 cross-file data contracts (FORMAT/NAME/VALUE/PATH), MF5 data_file fallback, MF6 AIAP.md sync + node count precision (±3 YELLOW, >3 RED), MF7 routing param symmetry, MF8 semantic operation consistency, MF9 cross-module invocation (bidirectional), MF10 system_prompt consistency, MF11 Error strategy consistency, MF12 tools union, MF13 persisted data schema versioning, MF14 cross-module field consumer validation, MF15 output contract enforcement, MF16 tool directory consistency (Pattern G), MF17 mandatory output completeness. MF20 cross-module call parameter match, MF23 name field version sync, MF24 protocol document consistency (3+ gaps → RED), MF25 tool integration completeness, MF26 enum registry consistency, MF27 new module dependency symmetry, MF28-MF38 extended checks (summary length, agent card consistency, etc). MF-INSIGHTS orphan file check. Aggregate: per-MF PASS/YELLOW/RED. Any MF RED → traffic RED.
ThreeDimTest.step9: MERMAID STRUCTURAL CHECKS: (1) Valid Mermaid flowchart syntax (graph TD/LR). (2) All node references resolve in edges. (3) Entry node present, at least one end node ((End)). (4) No orphan nodes without edges. (5) Sub_mermaid keys match delegation targets.
ThreeDimTest.constraints: Tier detection is deterministic. Auxiliary weight capped at 0.30. All MF checks mandatory for Pattern B+. TOOL-COMPUTED checks use exact comparison. Score 1-5 per item. [PROHIBIT]: No scoring without reading quality_checklist criteria definition first — quality_checklist MUST be loaded (via LoadChecklist or ReadTemplate) before any dimension scoring begins. Each check item must have tool-verified evidence, not cognitive-only scoring. [INDEPENDENT_AGENT] This function MUST be executed in a separate Agent context from code generation. The Agent receives only file paths, not evolution history. Cognitive scores max 4.5/5. SUB-DIMENSION MANDATORY (Cycle 196 finding): output.dimension_scores.C MUST contain exactly 7 keys (c1..c7), I MUST contain 13 keys (i1..i13), D MUST contain 10 keys (d1..d10). Approximation ('C≈4.5', 'I~4.7') without per-sub-dim keys is PROHIBITED — each sub-dim MUST have its own 1-5 numeric score AND a 1-sentence rationale referencing evidence (file path or tool output). If a sub-dim cannot be scored from evidence, write score=3.0 + rationale='insufficient_evidence: <what was missing>' explicitly; never silently omit. Consumers (QualityGate DIMENSION_FLOOR, SEMANTIC REGRESSION DETECTION score_drop>0.3) rely on per-sub-dim granularity — aggregating to C/I/D only breaks those gates. Cycle 196 observation: ReviewPresent displayed 'C~4.5 I~4.7 D~4.5' without c1-c7/i1-i13/d1-d10 breakdown; this qualifies as PARTIAL (complete<100%) not PASS. PRE-FINALIZE DEFERRED SCORING (P23, Cycle 198 finding): When ThreeDimTest runs in Generate2 context (pipeline_stage='generate2', BEFORE ReviewFinalize), D6 VERSION SYNC and D10 DESCRIPTION reference governance files (AIAP.md, agent_card.json, quality_baseline.json) that ReviewFinalize updates LATER — so at Generate2 time those files still show the OLD version. Do NOT penalize D6/D10 against old-state mismatch (this systematically clips every evolution by ~0.044 weighted: Cycle 198 observed 4.669→4.625 delta entirely from this timing artifact, NOT from real quality change). Instead mark as deferred: emit dimension_scores.D.D6 = {score: 4.5, deferred: true, note: 'pre-Finalize: governance files at old version; reconciled in ReviewFinalize.step3'} and dimension_scores.D.D10 = same shape. Neutral 4.5 (S-baseline assumed-compliant) is used in Generate2 weighted computation; ReviewFinalize.step3 re-measures and overwrites with actual post-update score. D8 SUMMARY is NOT deferred (true content-level constraint, score the real value). Consumers (QualityGate score_drop regression, SEMANTIC REGRESSION DETECTION) MUST skip deferred=true items when comparing vs old_version weighted to avoid false positives. Authoritative final score = ReviewFinalize's recomputed weighted (stored in quality_baseline.evolution_history.{version}.weighted_score). DIMENSION CALIBRATION ANCHORS (v2.33.0, per RF-7 inter-rater reliability — Cohen's kappa >= 0.60 threshold): To improve scoring consistency across pipeline runs, each dimension uses behavioral anchors for the 1 (weak) and 5 (strong) boundaries. Cognitive anchors: C1 weak=identifiers are single-letter or ambiguous abbreviations, C1 strong=all identifiers are self-describing English phrases matching domain vocabulary. C3 weak=multiple dead branches or unreachable nodes, C3 strong=every node reachable, all paths terminate at endNode, no cycles. Intrinsic anchors: I2 weak=no input guards present, raw user input reaches tool calls, I2 strong=all 5 guard types (type/size/injection/path/encoding) present with explicit rejection messages. I3 weak=Error fields missing or contain only 'retry', I3 strong=every Error field has retry->circuit-breaker->fallback->inform chain with typed error classification. Detail anchors: D1 weak=functions have single step with vague instructions, D1 strong=each function has numbered steps with actionable verb phrases and measurable outcomes. D3 weak=Error fields cover only happy path, D3 strong=Error fields enumerate all failure modes with typed classification and distinct recovery strategies. These anchors are reference calibration points — actual scoring uses the full quality_checklist criteria. Scoring without consulting anchors is PERMITTED but discouraged for dimensions with known inter-rater variance (C5, I2, D4). MULTI-AGENT VALIDATION CHAIN PATTERN: ThreeDimTest operates as part of a multi-agent validation chain (write/critique/test/validate) consistent with 2026 agentic pipeline best practices — one agent generates code, an independent agent scores quality, downstream agents validate and simulate. This layered approach addresses the 1.7x logical bug rate in AI-generated code (CodeRabbit 2026). Quality is the #1 production barrier (32% cite), observability at 89% adoption. Circuit breakers and audit trails are table stakes for agentic pipelines.
ThreeDimTest.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(TrafficClassify) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

TrafficClassify.step1: [ASSERT] ThreeDimTest executed. Required: threedim_results.  | GREEN — auto-apply silently: Error field addition, input validation addition, boundary rule text, minor formatting.
TrafficClassify.step2: YELLOW — auto-apply + report: restructuring suggestions, metabolic cleaning, missing constraint additions.
TrafficClassify.step3: RED — pause for human: fractal restructuring, parameter space changes, flow redesign, grade drop >=2 levels. CROSS-FILE GRANULARITY: version/id-only changes across files -> GREEN. Same-module internal changes (e.g. generate + its helper) -> YELLOW. Cross-module data contract changes (e.g. research output format affects generate input) -> RED. AIAP-TOOLS CLASSIFICATION: trust_level violation in aiap_tools/ -> RED. governance_hash mismatch in aiap_tools/ -> RED. circular dependency detected -> RED. permissions excess in aiap_tools/ -> RED. sub-program missing optional fields -> YELLOW. recursion depth approaching limit (depth=2) -> YELLOW.
TrafficClassify.step4: Generate traffic_report: { green:[...], yellow:[...], red:[...], dim_grades:{C,I,D} }.
TrafficClassify.constraints: Cross-file classification per CROSS-FILE GRANULARITY rules in step3. Score impact >=1.0 -> RED.
TrafficClassify.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(PostGenCheck) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

PostGenCheck.step1: [ASSERT] TrafficClassify executed. Required: traffic_report.  | Check traffic_report for unresolved RED items. If present -> PauseHuman.
PostGenCheck.step2: Display summary: 'Green N | Yellow N | Red N | C:{g} I:{g} D:{g} | weighted:{score}'.
PostGenCheck.constraints: RED items only gate. Weighted score threshold is checked by main.aisop.json QualityGate after generation completes.
PostGenCheck.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(PauseHuman) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

PauseHuman.step1: [ASSERT] PostGenCheck executed. Required: red_items.  | Present each RED item: what/why/proposed-change/before-after. SUB_MERMAID DECOMPOSITION DISPLAY: when RED item has severity='RED_WITH_FIX' and contains sub_mermaid_proposal, present enhanced view: (a) PATTERN — show detected decomposition pattern (FAN_OUT/SEQUENTIAL/CONDITIONAL) with explanation, (b) BEFORE — show current Mermaid graph with node count and highlight nodes to be extracted, (c) AFTER MAIN — show proposed main Mermaid with SubRef delegation nodes replacing extracted clusters, (d) AFTER SUB — show each proposed sub-graph Mermaid with Entry routing and handler nodes, (e) NODE COUNT COMPARISON — table showing before (N nodes) vs after (main: M nodes + sub1: K1 nodes + sub2: K2 nodes), (f) FUNCTION MIGRATION — list which functions move from main to sub-graph, (g) RISK ASSESSMENT — note any cross-references that may break.
PauseHuman.step2: Wait: approve, reject (continue with warning), or modify. For sub_mermaid_proposal: [Apply] executes decomposition via ApplyFixes.step2, [Reject] keeps current structure with fractal_exempt advisory, [Modify] allows user to adjust split points before applying.
PauseHuman.constraints: Never auto-approve RED items. Sub_mermaid decomposition proposals MUST show full before/after diff preview before user decision.
PauseHuman.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(SaveDraft) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

ApplyFixes.step1: [ASSERT] PostGenCheck or PauseHuman executed. Required: traffic_report, human_decisions.  | GREEN fixes: auto-apply non-breaking improvements. YELLOW fixes: apply with logging. RED fixes: only if human-approved via PauseHuman.
ApplyFixes.step2: SUB_MERMAID DECOMPOSITION APPLY (when traffic_report has RED_WITH_FIX, fix_type='sub_mermaid_decomposition', human approved): (1) Backup current aisop. (2) Replace main Mermaid. (3) Add sub_mermaid graphs with entry functions. (4) Add delegate functions to main. (5) Set fractal_exempt. (6) Migrate handler functions from main to sub-graph. (7) Verify all graphs <=15 nodes — fail → rollback. POST-SPLIT: verify each graph <=16 nodes, recursive check for nested sub-delegates. Log sub_mermaid_applied results.
ApplyFixes.step3: FIX ORDER: isolated → local per-function → cross-function one-at-a-time. MULTI-FILE: sub-modules first, main last.
ApplyFixes.step4: VERSION RULES: modify_only→patch+1, level_c_only→patch+1, level_b→minor+1, level_a→major+1, mixed→highest wins. NAME FIELD VERSION SYNC: update version pattern in name field to match new version. Record name_version_synced in pipeline_metadata.
ApplyFixes.step5: DESCRIPTION HYGIENE: >2 version history entries → compress (keep current, collapse older). SUMMARY CONSISTENCY: verify summary reflects current capabilities, <=500 chars. DERIVED REFERENCE AUDIT: (a) name version sync, (b) Error text hardcoded counts vs actual, (c) menu/feature count sync, (d) cross-function reference existence check. SMART DESCRIPTION UPDATE: extract current capabilities → compute delta from LEVEL_B changes → update description + module descriptions. Safety gate: >50% change → YELLOW for human review.
ApplyFixes.step6: CUSTOM FIELD SEMANTIC CHECK: preserve verified_on, author, tags. If content changed beyond cosmetic, append advisory to description.
ApplyFixes.constraints: WRITE SAFETY: in-memory backup. ATOMIC: .tmp rename. Max 3 fix cycles.
ApplyFixes.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(SaveDraft) -> inform(User). IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_write_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

SaveDraft.step1: [ASSERT] PauseHuman or ApplyFixes executed. Required: draft_files, workspace_dir.  | DIRECTORY RULES: Modify -> save in-place at {workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/. Evolve -> save in-place (overwrite with .bak backup). Create -> create new directory {workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/. All output uses .aisop.json format with Mermaid flow.
SaveDraft.step2: Save .aisop.json files. Atomic write: .tmp then rename. Backup as .v{old}.bak. AIAP.md: prepare but NOT write — pass to ReviewFinalize. MULTI-FILE: save each changed file, skip unchanged. Output content_hash per file. WRITE LOGGING: { files_written, backups_created, atomic_success, bytes_written }. MODULE DESCRIPTION TEMPLATE: each row follows '| **{filename}** | {role} — {base_capabilities}, **{version_features}** | {tools} |'. Evolve: demote old version_features to base, add new as bold.
SaveDraft.step3: Output: { draft_path, test_report, traffic_report, pattern, version, weighted_score }.
SaveDraft.constraints: Unresolved RED -> prepend WARNING in draft metadata.
SaveDraft.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(ReflectionCheck) -> inform(User). IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_write_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

ReflectionCheck.step1: [ASSERT] SaveDraft executed. Required: saved_draft_path.  | Verify: all Mermaid nodes have functions? Any RED residuals? Evolution items implemented?
ReflectionCheck.step2: Generate reflection_notes for ReviewFlow. Residuals -> note for next cycle.
ReflectionCheck.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(endNode) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.
